def: adj. (f. -ī) & adv. This much or many, &c. ( = اتنا itnā, q.v.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَتَا (atā), from a Turkic language.

def: = اتنے itne, q.v.s.v. اتنا itnā.
s.m. Union, concord, intimate friendship; combination, league, compact, treaty.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِتِّحَاد (ittihād), borrowed from Arabic اِتِّحَاد (ittiḥād), from اِتَّحَدَ (ittaḥada).

def: s.m. Following, obeying; obedience, allegiance, fidelity ( = تابع داري tābiʻ-dārī, q.v.).

s.m. Concurrence, agreement, accord, correspondence, coincidence; equality; union, unity, concord, harmony, unison, conformity; amity, friendship, affection; similarity of disposition; assent, consent; concert, combination, confederation, conspiracy, collusion; probability, contingency, case, event; circumstance, incident, affair, particular; opportunity; accident, chance, lot, fortune
subs: v.n. To accord, agree together, harmonize; to suit, be opportune or favourable; to turn out well
v.n. To happen, chance, come to pass, occur, turn out, fall out, betide; to arise; to ensue
(-se), To be in accord (with); to be on terms of intimacy, friendship, &c. (with); to live peaceably together
adv. Concurrently, conjointly, together, in conjunction with, in communication with; by chance, accidentally, incidentally; occasionally, rarely; scarcely
To accord, agree, concur, unite; to form an intimacy or alliance (with, -se), to harmonize (with); to combine, conspire (against, -par); to co-operate
To be at accord, to agree, be agreed, be of one mind, be in harmony (with, -se); to correspond (to, -se); to growintimate, become fast friends.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِتِّفَاق (ittifāq), from Arabic اِتِّفَاق (ittifāq). First attested in c. 1618 as Middle Hindi اتفاق (atfaq).

def: adv. Accidentally, by chance; by the way, occasionally, rarely; scarcely.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اتفاقاً (ittifāqan), itself from Arabic اِتِّفَاقًا (ittifāqan).

def: s.m. Accidents, occurrences, events; ittifāqāt-i-ḥasana (and ittifāq-i-ḥasana), s.m. Agreeable accidents or surprises; unexpected good fortune, good luck.
adj. Concurring, accidental, casual, occasional, incidental.
adj. Concurring, accidental, casual, occasional, incidental.
s.m. Suspicion; censure; (in law) charge, imputation.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِتِّهَام (ittihām).

def: s.m. Avoiding, shunning, abstaining (from, -se); piety, abstinence.
s.m. Restraining one's senses; abstaining, fasting (as in the month of Ramazān).

ety: inf. n. viii of طلع 'to rise, ascend, appear'
def: s.f. Manifesting, declaring; announcement, notice, information, intimation, advice, notification; knowledge, cognizance, insight, acquaintance
subs: (se), To inform or apprize (of), acquaint (with); to report; to announce, notify; to intimate; to make known; to refer (to); to direct
s.m. A written notification or declaration, written notice; a summons or citation
(-par), To be known (to); to come to the knowledge or cognizance (of)
s.f. Receipt or acknowledgment of a notification, notice, or intimation, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِطِّلَاع (ittilā'), borrowed from Arabic اِطِّلَاع (iṭṭilāʕ), from اِطَّلَعَ (iṭṭalaʕa).

def: adv. By way of announcement or intimation; for information.
adj. That gives intimation or information; for the purpose of announcing, apprising, or giving notice.
s.m. Description; praise; qualification; excellence.
s.m. Conjunction, contact; contiguity, neighbourhood; connection, adhesion, union, attachment.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِتِّصال (ittisāl), from Arabic اِتِّصَال (ittiṣāl).

def: s.m. Sunday.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit आदित्यवार (ādityavāra).

def: adj. & s.m. Et cœtera, and so forth; and other, and others.
adj. Pertaining to the rest.
conj. Thus, like, in like manner as; as, in some way, almost, scarcely.
wety: From Classical Persian او (ō).

r: ʻiwaẓ, ke, ʻiwaẓ denā, ko, ʻiwaẓ lenā, se, badlā lenā, ʻiwaẓ-muʻāwaẓa, muʻāwaẓ
def: s.m. Interchange, exchange, barter
subs: or baʻiwaẓ, adv. As a substitute (for, -ke), in lieu (of), in exchange (for), in return, in satisfaction, as compensation or indemnity; mutatis mutandis; in supersession (of).

def: s.m. = ʻiwaẓ, q.v.; — adv. As a substitute; by way of compensation, or return, &c.
adj. & s.m. Officiating, acting (for), in charge; — a substitute; a person officiating or acting (for another), a locum tenens
subs: To give or provide a substitute
To receive or accept a substitute
To act as a substitute, to officiate (for).

ety: for A. عيادة, inf. n. of عود 'to come repeatedly, to visit'
def: s.f. Visiting (of the sick)
subs: (-kī), To visit (a sick person); to make inquiries after the health (of a sick person).

def: s.m. Stag; deer, hart; wild goat.

ety: for A. ايلة, rt. آل for اول
def: s.f. Rule, government, dominion.

ety: inf. n. of عاير 'to measure, compare,' &c., iii of عير 'to come and go, to move to and fro'
def: s.m. A standard (of measure, weight, or fineness); mark, proof, test, touchstone, assay.
(ʻiyāẕaṉ, acc. of ʻiyāẕ, inf. n. of عوذ 'to seek protection,' &c.), 'By flying for refuge to God,' (which is equivalent to) God avert, Heaven forefend.
adv. When; lo, behold.

ety: inf. n. iv of اذي 'To be annoyed,' &c.
def: s.f. Annoyance, molestation, vexation, pain, trouble, distress, harm, hurt, injury; (syn. dukh)
subs: īẕā denā (-ko), To annoy, molest, vex, &c.
īzā-rasān, adj. & s.m. Annoying, molesting, &c.; — one who gives annoyance, &c.
īẕā rasānī, s.f. The occasioning of annoyance, injury, &c.

def: s.m. Increase, augmentation, addition

ety: inf. n. iv of ضعف 'to exceed'
def: s.m. Doubling, duplication.
s.m. Addition, annexation; increase, augmentation; increment, adjunct; accretion; surplus, excess; attribute; allowance, scholarship

ety: for A. اضافة, inf. n. iv of ضيف 'to incline' (to)
def: s.f. Lit. 'the annexation'; (in Gram.), prefixing a noun to a noun, so that the former governs the latter in the genitive case (e.g. g̠ulām-i-Zaid, 'the slave of Zaid'); the connection of a noun so prefixed with its complement; the short vowel kasra between two nouns in the formation of the genitive case, or on the addition of an adjective; addition, adjunct, &c. = iẓāfa, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِضَافَت (izāfat), from Arabic إِضَافَة (ʔiḍāfa).

ety: inf. n. iv of زال for زول 'to depart,' &c.
def: s.m. Removing; annulling; taking away; removal; abolition; (in Gram.) elision of a letter or of a vowel point from a word
subs: v.t. To deprive of virginity, to deflower; to commit a rape
s.m. Robbing of one's good name, defamation
v.t. To remove, annul, abolish, take away, deprive; to put away.

def: s.m. pl. (of ʻaz̤m), Bones; — s.m. pl. (of ʻaz̤īm) The great.

ety: inf. n. iv of ذعن 'to be tractable'
def: s.m. Obedience, submission; confidence, belief.
s.f. Drawers, trousers (syn. pāʼe-jāma)
subs: s.m. The string with which drawers āre tied, trouser-strings
(-ke), To lay hands on the trouser-strings; to evince desire for, or to contemplate, carnal intercourse
(-meṅ), To run the string into drawers

adj. m. (f. -kī ḍhīlī), Ready for carnal intercourse, libidinous, lecherous, wanton, of easy virtue (a woman); — s.m. A rake, debauchee; — izār-band-kī ḍhīlī, s.f. A woman of easy virtue, an unchaste woman
s.m. Connection through a wife or mistress; petticoat interest
(-ko), To treat with levity, to disregard, feel no care or concern (for); to be fearless (of), indifferent (to, — used by women).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ازار (izār), from Arabic إِزَار (ʔizār).

def: s.m. The cheek, face.

ety: inf. n. viii of زحم 'to push or press against'
def: s.m. Concourse, crowd, throng, pressing or rushing together of people.

ety: inf. n. viii of زاج for زوج 'to pair or couple'
def: s.m. Marriage; wedding.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِزْدِوَاج (izdiwāj), from Arabic اِزْدِوَاج (izdiwāj).

ety: inf. n. viii of زاد for زيد 'to increase'
def: s.m, Increase, augmentation, addition.

ety: inf. n. iv of ظہر 'to appear, be manifest,' &c.
def: s.m. Manifestation, revelation, disclosure, demonstration, publication, display, declaration; statement, declaration in a court of justice or before police officers; information; examination of a witness, deposition, evidence, written testimony
subs: s.m. Deposition in writing; written testimony (opp. of iz̤hār-i-zabānī)
v.t. To give publicity (to); to make a deposition; to depose, testify, give evidence, bear witness (to)
s.m. A complimentary (but illegal) fee paid to the writer of a deposition
s.m. Legal declaration; statement conformable to law
v.t. To disclose, reveal, expose; to publish, make known, notify; to declare, affirm; to describe, explain
(-kā), To take the deposition (of); to examine (a witness)
s.m. A deposition writer, an officer of the court who takes down depositions.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِظْهَار (izhār), from Arabic إِظْهَار (ʔiẓhār).

ety: inf. n. iv of ذکر 'to remember'
def: s.m. Reminding; exhorting to obedience; remembering, praising (God).
s.m. Permission, leave, concession of liberty (to do a thing); consent of two contracting parties (as in a marriage contract)
subs: s.m. (Among Mohammadans) permission to depart given after the reading of prayers over the dead
s.m. Distribution of property by a testator, a will.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِذْن (izn), from Arabic إِذْن (ʔiḏn).

def: s.m. Name of the angel of death.

ety: inf. n. viii of ضرب 'to beat'
def: s.m. Agitation, perturbation, restlessness, distraction, anxiety, anguish, trouble, chagrin; precipitation; flurry.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِضْطِرَاب (iztirāb), from Arabic اِضْطِرَاب (iḍṭirāb, “disturbance; disorder”).

def: s.f. Agitation; hurry, impatience, precipitation.

ety: inf. n. viii of ضرّ 'to injure'
def: Violence, compulsion, constraint; agitation, perturbation; despair, the last extremity.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic اِضْطِرَار (iḍṭirār).

ety: inf. n. viii of ضرّ 'to injure'
def: Violence, compulsion, constraint; agitation, perturbation; despair, the last extremity.

ety: inf. n. of عزّ 'to be mighty, or glorious,' &c.
def: s.m. Might, greatness, grandeur, glory, dignity, majesty, excellence; — adj. Mighty, great, giorious, &c.

ety: for A. عزة, inf. n. of عزّ; see ʻizz
def: s.f. Might, power, grandeur, glory, honour, dignity, respect, esteem, reputation, good name
subs: (kisī-kī), To strip off the honour (of anyone), to dishonour, &c. ( = ʻizzat lenā)
(kisī-kī), To destroy the honour (of), to dishonour, &c. ( = ʻizzat lenā, q.v.)
adj. & s.m. Powerful, holding high rank, honourable, respectable, respected, esteemed; — a man of rank or honour, &c.; a man highly respected; a respectable man
(-ko), To confer honour or dignity (on), to do honour (to), to dignify, ennoble, exalt
(apnī), To preserve (one's) honour, or dignity, or good name; to be held in esteem
or ʻizzat-ke pīćhe paṛnā (kisī-kī), To be bent on ruining the honour or reputation (of one), to be bent on the disgrace (of)
(-kī), To render honour (to), to pay respect (to), to honour, dignify, &c.
(-kī), To deprive (one) of honour, &c., to dishonour, disgrace; to abuse, to insult; to put to shame; to destroy the reputation (of), take away the character (of); to debauch, violate, ravish
or ʻizzat-meṅ farq ānā (-kī), To have (one's) reputation or character sullied, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عِزَّت (izzat), borrowed from Arabic عِزَّة (ʕizza), from عَزَّ (ʕazza).

ety: Pehl. gāś; Zend gātu; S. gātu, rt. gā
def: s.f. Place (syn. gāh)
subs: adj. & adv. In place or out of place; right or wrong; at all times indiscriminately
s.m. A necessary office, a privy (syn. pai-ḵẖāna)
s.m. A locum tenens, a deputy, a vicegerent; a successor
s.f. Sitting in the place or room (of); lieutenancy, vicegerency; succession
s.f. A carpet, mat, or cloth on which prayers are said.

def: intj. Be off! away! avaunt!

r: jā, jo, Hindī jis, jā-su, jis-se, jā-hi, jis-ko
def: the inflected base, in Braj, of the rel. pron. jo, and = the Hindī jis; — e.g. jā-su, From whom, from which; whereby ( = jis-se); jā-hi, To whom, to whomsoever ( = jis-ko).
s.m. A muzzle (for large cattle), ox-muzzle; a net (for fruit-trees); a kind of grass.

ety: Prk. जेम्वहिं or जेम्वइं loc. sing. of जेम्व = S. यावत्
def: conj. (correl. to tab), When, at the time when; as soon as; in case; since (temporal and causal)
subs: jab-talak; (dialec.) jab-taʼīṅ, jab-toṛī, adv. Till when; till such time as, until; whilst, while; as long as, so long as; by that (or which) time
adj. (f. -ī), Of that time; past, previous, last
jab-kā-jab, adv. At the time when; at the proper moment
jab-kabhī, adv. When at any time, whenever
adv. & conj. At the time when, when; while; since (temp. & caus.)
adv. Now and then, at times; perpetually, always; never, not at all
adv. At the very time or moment, whenever, as soon as; — jab-hī, jab-hī-to, conj. Hence (it is, or was), on that account, therefore.
wety: From اب (ab, “now”). Cognate with Punjabi ਜਦੋਂ (jadõ) / جدوں (jadõ).

def: s.m. A quiver (for arrows).
adj. Stiff, rigid; awkward, clumsy; heavy.
adj. Ill-formed, ill-shaped, clumsy, ungainly, ugly, &c. = jabaddā.
v.n. To be filled; to be turned down or back; to be folded.
adj. = jabaddiyā, q.v.
s.m. A mountain, hill.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic جَبَل (jabal).

def: s.m. An amethyst.
adj. & s.m. Cowardly, pusillanimous, timid, abject; — a coward.
s.f. Oppression, injury ( = jabr); — adj. Compelled, forced, compulsory, of force.

ety: from jabbār; the termination -ūt is Aramaic, and = Arabic-īyat
def: s.f. Omnipotence; the world or sphere of omnipotence; heaven; dominion.
adj. & s.m. Omnipotent, mighty; — a conqueror; a revenger; a tall palm tree; Orion.
s.f. A species of rice (grown in Rohelkhand).
s.m. The jaw (cf. jabhṛā, jabṛā).
s.m. The forehead ( = jabīn)
subs: adj. Rubbing the forehead (on the ground), beseeching, begging earnestly; — jabha-sāʼī, s.f. The act of rubbing the forehead on the ground, beseeching, entreaty.

def: adv. = jab-hī, q.v.s.v. jab.
s.m. = jabṛā, q.v.

ety: Prk. जंभिआ; S. जम्भ+इका; see jāb
def: s.f. A muzzle (for small cattle).
s.f. dim. of jāb, q.v.
s.f. The forehead ( = peshānī; māthā)
subs: (or sāʼe), jabīn-farsā (or -farsāʼe), adj. Rubbing the forehead (on the ground in adoration or supplication).

def: adj. & s.m. Despotic, tyrannical; — despot, tyrant (cf. jabbār).

ety: inf. n. of جبر 'to set (a bone
def: s.m. Compulsion, constraint, coercion; force, power, strength; violence, outrage, oppression; — the reduction of fractions to integrals; the addition of something for the purpose of reparation, throwing in something by way of compensation
subs: v.n. To endure oppression, &c.; to suffer; to take trouble or pains
adv. By force, forcibly, &c. ( = ba-jabr; jabraṉ)
(-par), To use or employ force, &c. (on), to compel, constrain, coerce; to press, bear forcibly (on)
s.m. Reparation or compensation for loss or injury; making up for a deficiency (in quality, &c.)

def: s.m. (prov.) = jabṛā, q.v.
s.m. The lower jaw-bone; the jaw, or the portion of the face from the corners of the mouth to the jaws (cf. jabhā)
= A جبرائيل jabrāʼīl, dial. vars. of jabraʼīl, q.v.
= A جبراعل jabrāʻil, dial. vars. of jabraʼīl, q.v.
(Heb. גַּבְרִיאֵל), prop. n. lit. 'Servant, or man, of God'; the archangel Gabriel.
adv. By force, forcibly, compulsively, violently ( = ba-jabr; zabardastī)
subs: (-ko), To enrol or enlist by force, to press, impress
adv. By force and violence; willingly or unwillingly, nolens volens.

def: s.f. The jaw
dial. vars. of next, q.v.
a dial. var. of jabraʼīl, q.v.
s.m. Conversation, talk, conference; reply, rejoinder.
s.f. = jāb-sāl; — adj. Ready at reply, ready-witted; fluent, voluble.
s.m. = jāṅć, q.v.
s.f. A woman who has recently given birth to a child (so called until the fortieth day after her delivery), a lying-in woman
subs: s.m. A song sung on the occasion of the birth of a child.

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Tried, proved, experimented on; experimental

ety: rt. of jāćnā, q.v.+āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअ+ट्ट = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं; and āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+ इका
def: s.f. Test, trial, examination, proof, investigation, scrutiny; valuation, appraisement.
s.m. Petitioner, solicitor, beggar, mendicant; suitor, candidate; a wandering musician ( = jājak).
s.m. = jājkī, q.v.

ety: jāćak+Prk. प्पणं = त्वणं = S. त्वं (Vedic. त्वनं
def: s.m. Begging, mendicancy.
s.f. Asking, soliciting, begging, &c.

ety: rt. of jāćnā, q.v.+āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअ+ट्ट = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं; and āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+ इका
def: s.f. Test, trial, examination, proof, investigation, scrutiny; valuation, appraisement.
v.n. = jaćnā; — v.t. = jāṅćnā, and jāćnā.
s.m. (colloq.) = jaj, or yajna, qq.v.

part. Asked, solicited, prayed for, begged, obtained by begging; — s.m. A borrowed article or thing: (in Law) a particular form of deposit whereby the holder is allowed the use of the article deposited.
v.t. To ask, beg, solicit, pray, request, desire, entreat, implore; to want, need.
v.t. = jāṅćnā, q.v.

ety: fr. the trans., jāćnā or jāṅćnā
def: v.n. To be tested or tried, be examined, be investigated; to be valued, be appraised, be estimated, be reckoned at its worth, be rated; to be judged, be esteemed.
adv. (correl. tadā), When, whenever; as, as soon as, &c. ( = jab, q.v.)
conj. = jadī, q.v.
s.f. Crisp or curly hair; a ringlet, a lock of hair.
adv. = jad, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جدا (judā, “separate, apart”). Compare Punjabi جُدا (judā) / ਜੁਦਾ (judā), Gujarati જુદું (judũ).

def: s.m. Way, road, pathway, highway; the right road; manner, practice (cf. jādaj).
s.f. = jāʼe-dād, q.v.
s.m. = jāda, q.v.
s.m. Contention, disputation, altercation, fighting; fight, battle, encounter (cf. jidāl).
conj. See s.v. jad and jadī.
s.m. A descendant of Yadu; a name of Kr̤ishṇa (as descended from Yadu); a stock of cattle
subs: s.m. 'Lord of the Yādavas,' a title of Kr̤ishṇa
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جَادُو (jādū), from Middle Persian 𐭩𐭠𐭲𐭥𐭪𐭠 (yʾtʿkʾ /⁠ǰādūg⁠/, “wizardry; wizard”), from Old Persian 𐎹𐎠𐎬𐎢 (y-a-tu-u /⁠yātu⁠/), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *yaHtúš.

def: s.f. pl. (of jadwal), Lines, marginal lines, columns (in a book, &c.); rivulets.
s.m. Unmeaning expression, nonsense; so so.
s.m. Fortune, good fortune; riches, competence, prosperity, happiness; — greatness, majesty, dignity, glory (of God); — a grandfather, an ancestor (pl. ajdād).
adj. Ancestral, belonging to ancestors, paternal (estate, &c.).
adv. (prov.) When, &c. = jad, q.v.
s.m. = jaṛhan, q.v.
s.m. A kid; a young he-goat; the sign Capricorn; the star a of Ursa minor, the pole-star.
conj. If, in case that; whether; if perchance
subs: vulg. jad-api (S. yady-api), conj. Even if, even though, though, although, notwithstanding that.

def: (part. n. from جدّ 'to cut off'), adj. lit. 'Newly cut off'; new, fresh, recent, modern.
s.m. Name of a low caste in villages (employed in menial offices; syn. kamīn; śudra).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جَادُو (jādū), from Middle Persian 𐭩𐭠𐭲𐭥𐭪𐭠 (yʾtʿkʾ /⁠ǰādūg⁠/, “wizardry; wizard”), from Old Persian 𐎹𐎠𐎬𐎢 (y-a-tu-u /⁠yātu⁠/), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *yaHtúš.

def: s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts.

ety: Old P. yādu, yātu; Pehl. yātuk; Zend yātu; S. yātu
def: s.m. Magic, enchantment, incantation, charm, conjuring, juggling
subs: (-ko), To practise incantations, magic, &c.; to enchant, charm, bewitch, conjure
s.m. Magician, sorcerer, wizard, enchanter, conjurer, juggler
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جَادُو (jādū), from Middle Persian 𐭩𐭠𐭲𐭥𐭪𐭠 (yʾtʿkʾ /⁠ǰādūg⁠/, “wizardry; wizard”), from Old Persian 𐎹𐎠𐎬𐎢 (y-a-tu-u /⁠yātu⁠/), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *yaHtúš.

def: adj. Of or belonging to the family of Jadu
s.f. A rivulet, streamlet (natural or artificial); a ruled line (round a page), marginal line; column, table (of a book); an astronomical table
subs: jadwal-kash, s.f. A rule, consisting of a card of pasteboard and threads on which the paper is pressed and thus becomes ruled.

def: adj. Marked with lines (a book, &c.), lined, ruled.
s.m. Zedoary, Curcuma zedoaria (syn. nir-bisī, or narbasī).
s.m. Coldness, frigidity; stiffness, inactivity, apathy, sluggishness, insensibility; stolidity, dulness of intellect, stupidity, folly.
s.f. Place; room; ground, &c. = jā (of which it is the usual form in the construct state)
subs: The time or place of opposition or objection; room for objection
Place of anxiety or fear; cause for apprehension
Ground of excuse, or of complaint or objection
s.f. Place, station, dwelling, residence (cf. jagah)
adj. Taking a place; taking hold, fixed; efficacious; — s.f. = jāgīr, q.v.
Place of retribution; cause for retribution. (For other comp., as jāʼe-nishīn, &c., see s.v. jā.)

def: s.f. Place, station; appointment, employment, service; consignment; an assignment on land (for the maintenance of troops, or of an establishment, or of a person); estate, property, effects, assets, funds, resources; the means or capabilities of a district in respect of revenue
subs: s.f. An assignment of revenue in perpetuity (with a reservation of a certain amount of rent and of other Government claims)
Movable or personal property, personalty, personal effects, goods, chattels
Property which is the subject of a will.

def: s.f. See s.v. jāʼe.
s.f. = jāgīr, q.v.; — adj. See s.v. jāʼe.
s.f. Mace ( = jāwatrī).
s.m. Nutmeg, Myristica moschata.
s.m. Name of a tribe of sūraj-bansī Rājpūts.
s.f. (orig. 'roughness or rudeness,' but, by the Persians, used in the sense of) Oppression, violence, cruelty, injury, injustice, hardship
subs: jafā-jū, jafā-shěʻār, jafā-kār, adj. & s.m. Oppressive, tyrannical, unjust, cruel; — a tormentor, oppressor, tyrant
adj. Enduring oppression or hardship; hard-working, energetic

def: s.m. A river; — a proper name (of a man).

ety: rel. n. fr. jaʻfar, a man's name
def: s.f. The purest kind of gold; — the yellow flower Linum trigynum (syn. gul-ě-ashrafī); — a kind of cupola; — a sort of screen (the interstices of the frame being lozenged); lattice-work; a bamboo frame (in these senses perhaps a corruption of the A. ẓafīra).
s.m. A company, troop, or body of men or people (syn. guroh); — juff, Anything hollow; an old worn-out man.
s.m. A company, troop, body ( = jaff).
s.m. (lit. 'a lamb or kid that has begun to pasture'), The art of divination (by examining the entrails of a lamb, &c.); the art of making amulets or charms
s.m. A sacrifice, offering, oblation.
wety: Deverbal from جاگْنا (jāgnā, “to wake up”).

def: s.m. The world, earth, universe
subs: s.m. 'The support of the universe,' the Supreme Being
s.m. 'Preserver of the world,' the Supreme Being; a sovereign or king
s.m. 'Lord of the universe'; an epithet of Vishnu, and of Śiva; a king or prince
jag-tāraṇ, jag-trātā, s.m. The Saviour of the world; the Ganges (according to the Hindū religion)
s.m.f. An epithet of any living creature
s.f. 'Mother of the universe'; any chief goddess (as Pārvatī, &c.)
s.m. An epithet of the Supreme Being as the giver of life
jag-rāy, jag-rāʼī, jag-rāyā, s.m. 'Monarch or Lord of the Universe,' the Supreme Being
s.m. 'Upholder of the world,' the Supreme Being; (in Myth.) any chief deity
s.m. 'Root or foundation of the universe'; God
s.f. An epithet of any very charming woman
s.m. Inhabiting the universe, dwelling in the world
s.m. An inhabitant of the world
jag-vanćak, s.m. 'Deceiver of the world,' a great hypocrite
adj. & s.m. Of world-wide renown; a person of world-wide renown
s.m. The way of the world, universal custom
s.f. Cause of universal or general laughter; whatever causes general laughter
vulg. jag-gīsar (jag+īśvara), s.m. 'Lord of the universe'; an epithet of Vishṇu, and of Śiva.
wety: Borrowed from English jug.

def: s.m. A feast, an entertainment; a sacrifice or religious ceremony in which oblations are presented
subs: (S. yajnā+upavīta), s.m. The sacrificial thread originally worn by the Brāhman, the Kshatriya, and the Vaisya, as distinctive of their castes, but now confined to the Brāhmanical order
s.m. One who is invested with the sacrificial thread.
wety: Borrowed from English jug.

def: s.m. = jāgaran, q.v.
wety: Deverbal from جاگْنا (jāgnā, “to wake up”).

def: s.m. A designation of the Bhāṭ tribe of Hindūs.
s.f. (prov.) = jagah, q.v.
part. adj. contrac. of jāgā, q.v.
s.f. corr. of jagah, q.v.
adj. Awake; — (jāg, q.v.+S. कः) s.m. Waking ( = jāg)
s.f. = S جگديش जगदीश jagad-īś, s.m. See s.v. jagat.
s.m. = S جگدنبا जगदम्बा jagad-ambā, s.f. See s.v. jagat.

ety: S. ज+गातु, by redupl.; cf. P. jā, and gāh, and jāʼe-gāh
def: s.f. Place, station, quarter, locality; room, stead; vacant space, blank; opening, vacancy, post, appointment, situation; fit occasion (as for anger, &c.)
subs: v.n. To leave a place; to leave a vacant space or blank
(-ko), To give place or room (to); to place; to make room (for), to lodge
jagah-se honā, v.n. To be in place, to be opportune or proper
jagah, adv. & postpn. In the place or room (of, -kī), instead (of).

def: s.f. Brightness, brilliancy, splendour, much light.
s.f. Brightness, brilliancy, splendour, much light.
s.m. (Pros.) An amphibrach.

ety: jagā˚ = Prk. जग्गाव(इ) or जग्गावे(इ); see jāgnā, of which it is the caus.
def: v.t. To waken, to rouse from sleep; to raise (the dead, or the wick of a lamp), to make bright or lively; to rouse, to stimulate
s.m. A tribe of Brāhman zamīndārs in Fatěhpūr.
s.m. See s.v. jagat.
s.m. Armour, coat of mail.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جِگَر (jigar).

def: s.m. Waking, wakefulness, vigil, sitting up at night (in a religious ceremony or at prayers), vespers.

ety: jāgar˚ = Prk. जागर(इ) = S. जागर्(ति), rt. जागृ
def: v.n. = jāgnā, q.v.
s.f. See jāgat.
s.f. Waking; keeping watch; watchfulness, vigilance.

ety: chargeable, in combination, to jagad, jagan, &c.
def: s.m. The world, the universe; men and animals, created things; — a buttress; a curb of masonry round a well; — adj. Movable, locomotive, transitory
subs: prop. jagad-ambā, jagad-ambikā, s.f. 'The mother of the world,' a name of Durgā or Pārvati
prop. jagad-ātmā, s.m. The soul of the world, the supreme spirit, Brahma
s.m. 'Stay or supporter of the universe,' the supreme spirit; — air, wind
adj. 'Enlightening the world'; an epithet of the Supreme
s.m. The sustaining or nourishing of the world
s.m. 'The breath of the world,' wind, air
s.f. The redemption of the world
adj. World-illumining, brilliant, bright; known to the whole world, universally celebrated
s.m. 'Parent of the universe,' an epithet of the Deity
s.m. 'Painer of the world,' a tyrant
s.m. 'Lord of the universe,' the Supreme Being
s.m. A great seṭh or banker; an epithet of a very wealthy class of men
s.m. The creator of the world, Brahma
s.m. The destruction of the world
prop. jagaḍ-guru, s.m. 'The teacher of the world'; an epithet of Brahma, of Vishnu, and of Śiva; a senior or elder in religion
adj. & s.m. Fascinating or bewildering the world; — one who bewilders or infatuates the world

s.m. 'Lord of the universe'; a name of Vishnu or Krishna; name of a celebrated idol, and of a temple and the surrounding district on the Coromandel coast, near Cuttack, in Orissa, where Vishnu is especially worshipped as Jagannath (and to the idol enshrined in which pilgrimages are made from all parts of India; see rath-jātrā)
s.m. The rice eaten at Jagannāth by pilgrims of all castes out of the same pot; (hence) something that may be eaten by all without loss of caste
s.m. The destruction of the world, the expiration of a jug or period of the world's existence

vulg. jagat-vandh, adj. 'To be praised or adored by the world'; an epithet of Krishna; — jagad-īs, jagdis, jagdīsā, jagad-īś, jagdīś, jagad-īśvar, s.m. 'Lord of the world or universe,' an epithet of Vishṇu, and of Śiva.
wety: From Sanskrit जगत (jagata).

def: s.f. & adj. The earth, &c. = jagat; — mankind, people; — a kind of metre
subs: s.f. The earth, world; sublunary things.

def: s.m. = jagneśwar, q.v.s.v. jagna; — and = jagad-īśvar, q.v.s.v. jagat.
s.m. = jagneśwar, q.v.s.v. jagna; — and = jagad-īśvar, q.v.s.v. jagat.
s.m. The hinder part, the buttock; the hip and loins; the pudenda, mons veneris; — rear-guard, reserve of an army.
s.f. (prov.) = jagah, q.v.
adj. Hindmost, hinder; last, latest; lowest; low, worst, vile, base; — s.m. A śūdra or man of the lowest caste
subs: adj. & s.m. Last-born, youngest; low-born; a younger brother; — a śūdra.

ety: S. जागरण; or Prk. जग्गडो, i.e. jagga˚ ( = jāg˚, see jāgnā)+ḍo = S. रः
def: s.m. Wakefulness (occasioned by apprehension, or disease; syn. jagnāʼī); vigils, vespers; coughing at night.

ety: jā, q.v.+gīr, rt. of giriftan 'to take'
def: s.f. Land and villages given by government as a reward for services or as a fee, a rent-free grant, a freehold, a pension (in land, &c.), a fief
subs: s.m. The holder of a jāgīr or fee, a feoffee, grantee; a holder of a perpetual tenure subject to a quit rent and service
s.f. A rent-free estate obtained as a grant in perpetuity
s.f. A grant of land, &c. attached to an office or dignity.

def: adj. Worthy of worship or sacrifice; proper, suitable, or fit for sacrifice
subs: s.f. 'Sacrificial country; the country of the Hindūs, that region (of Hindūstān) in which the sacrificial ceremonies can be duly performed, the country in which the black antelope is native.

ety: by redupl. of jag = S. जगम्, intens. fr. rt. गम्+S. कः
def: s.m. Brass-tinsel; very thin plates of brass used in decoration (cf. jagmagā).

ety: fr. jagjagānā; — āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअ+ट्ट् = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: s.f. Dazzling light, glitter, &c. = jagmagāhaṭ.
v.n. To dance or move about (light), to give a dazzling light, to glitter, &c. ( = jagmagānā).

ety: by redupl. fr. जगम्, intens. of rt. गम्
def: s.f. Dazzling light, glitter, &c. ( = jagmagāhaṭ, q.v.).
adj. Dazzling, brilliant, glittering, splendid.
s.f. Dazzling light, brilliance, glare; much light; glitter, splendour, lustre.

ety: see jagmag; — ā˚ = Prk. आव = S. आपि; nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To give a dazzling light, to glitter, shine
s.f. = jagmagāhaṭ, q.v.

ety: jāg˚ = Prk. जग्ग(इ) = S. जागर्(ति), rt. जागृ
def: v.n. To waken, be awake; to watch, keep vigil; to become vigilant, be on the alert; to be refreshed; to brighten
subs: v.n. To waken, wake up, be roused; to brighten up, &c.

def: s.m. A sacrifice ( = jag; jagya)
subs: s.m. The sacrificial or sacred thread worn by the Brahmanical order ( = janeʼo)
(S. yajna+īshvara), s.m. 'Lord of worship or of sacrifice,' an epithet of Vishṇu and of Śiva.

def: v.n. To be awake; to awake, wake up
subs: v.n. To wake up, rouse up.

ety: rt. of jagnā+āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइआ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. Wakefulness, &c. = jaghar.
s.m. See s.v. jagna.
s.f. A kind of small grain from which oil is extracted.
s.m. = jagna; jagya, qq.v.
s.m. See s.v. jag, and jagna.
adj. Awake; on the watch, vigilant (see jāgtā).
s.f. Coarse or brown sugar, refuse of sugar; treacle, molasses ( = guṛ); sugar of the palmyra tree.
adj. Watchful, attentive; awake.
adj. (f. -ī), Waking, awake; watching, vigilant; active, being in the full exercise of its power, virtue, or efficacy (as an idol, or a charm or spell, or a medicine, &c.); — s.m. One who is awake
subs: (S. jāgrat-jyotis), adj. Possessed of or exerting miraculous powers (as an idol in the full exercise of its divinity, or a mantra or medicine of ready virtue or power)
v.n. To remain or continue in a state of wakefulness, to keep awake.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Waking, awake.

ety: jāg, rt. of jāg-nā+ū = Prk. इउओ = S. (इ)तृ+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Waking, wakeful vigilant; one who awakens, one who is wakeful or vigilant.
v.t. To cause to wake, or to be roused.
s.m. Sacrifice, offering or oblation ( = jagna; jag)
subs: s.m. An animal for sacrifice, a victim; a horse
s.f. The completion or accomplishment of a sacrifice, the due performance of a sacrificial ceremony; obtaining the objects of a sacrifice
s.m. The sacrificial or sacred thread worn (criginally by the three first classes of Hindus, but now) by the Brahmanical order ( = jagna-sūtra)
s.f. A sacrificial hall; a house or place for keeping the sacrificial fire
s.m. A sacrificial act, a sacrificial rite or ceremony
(S. yajna+upa˚), s.m. = jagya-sūtra (also written jagnopabīt; jagopavīt, see s.v. jagna, and jag)
s.m. One who is invested with the sacrificial or sacred thread.

def: s.m. Sacrificer, the institutor of a sacrifice.
adj. Fit for sacrifice.

ety: Pehl. gāś; Zend gātu; S. gātu
def: s.f. Dignity, rank, high position; grandeur
subs: Rank and grandeur, dignity; splendour, magnificence, pomp; — jāh o-manṣab, Rank and position or dignity.

def: s.f. A plant, the flowers of which resemble those of the kadamb.
s.m. corr. of jahāz, q.v.
s.f. Imperfect knowledge, ignorance, barbarism, brutality.

r: jahān, jahāṅ, gehān, gīhān, jihān, gaetha
ety: Old P. gehān, gīhān, jihān; Pehl. gehān; Zend gaetha
def: s.m. The world
subs: s.m. Keeper or protector of the world; the Deity; a powerful monarch
adj. & s.m. World-seeing; an epithet of the Deity; (met.) the eye; a traveller
s.m. World-protection; protector or asylum of the world; his majesty; your majesty!
Long live the king! (the cry of the naqīb or herald who rides before the sovereign)
adj. World-inflaming; heating or warming the world (the sun)
s.m. Possessor or ruler of the world, a king
s.m. One who has seen the world, a great traveller; an experienced person
adj. & s.m. World-inflaming; — an incendiary; a fire-brand
adj. & s.m. Going round the world; — one who goes round the world, a great traveller
adj. & s.m. World-taking, world-subduing; — a great conqueror, a sovereign; name of the son and successor of the Emperor Akbar
adj. & s.f. Of or relating to Jahangīr; of or relating to a sovereign, royal, princely; — universal sway; — a sort of bracelet
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جهان (jahān).

def: adv. = jahāṅ-tahāṅ.

ety: i.e. jit+thān; Prk. जेत्तिअ+थाने; S. यावत्+स्थाने
def: adv. (correl. tahāṅ), Which place, where, in the place which; wherever, wheresoever
subs: adv. Here and there, all about; promiscuously; in some few spots or places
(or -kī-, or -ke-) tahāṅ, adv. Here, there, and everywhere; everywhere; — in the same place as before, without change of position or situation
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جهان (jahān).

def: s.m. The period of the total destruction of the world.
adj. & s.m. Relating to the world; worldly; — a person of this world, a human being.
s.m. pl. (of jahānī), Mortals, creatures, worldly people.
s.m. Hell; hell-fire; — Gehenna, the Valley of Hinnom.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جَهَنَّم (jahannam). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi جہنم (jhnm /⁠jahannam⁠/).

def: adj. Of or belonging to hell; hellish, infernal.

s.m. A ship; the tree of a camel's saddle, or the saddle and its appurtenances; the requisites, or equipments, or the paraphernalia, of a bride (which she carries to her husband's house, syn. jahez, q.v.); funeral apparatus; — the pudendum of a woman
subs: s.m. An iron ship; an iron-clad
adv. Aboard a ship, aboard, on board
To discharge cargo, to unload a ship
s.m. A ship itself, quite a ship (applied adjectively to any thing or person of great size)
To pilot a ship; — jahāz-ke utāre-kī jagah, A landing-place for ships, a port (syn. bandar)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جَهَاز (jahāz), borrowed from Arabic جَهَاز (jahāz).

def: adj. & s.m. Naval, nautical; — a sailor
subs: s.f. Trade by ship; carrying trade, commerce
jahāzī ḍākū, s.m. A pirate, a privateer
s.m. A greyhound (in this sense jahāzī is probably a corruption of the A. jahīz, 'fleet, swift')
s.m. Sailors; the crew (of a ship).

def: s.m. Labour, toil, exertion, effort, endeavour, struggle, painstaking, care, diligence, assiduity, earnestness
subs: v.n. To strive, to use effort, to endeavour, &c.

def: s.m. The paraphernalia of a bride, vestments and furniture of every kind which a bride brings to her husband's house; bride's portion, a dowry.
s.f. The great-flowered jasmine, Jasminum grandiflorum; a fire-work resembling the jasmine (cf. jūhī).
pron. (dialec.) = jahīṅ, and yah-ī, qq.v.
adj. Ignorant, illiterate, barbarous; uncivilized, rude, ill-mannered; silly, foolish; wrong (in conduct); — s.m. An ignorant person, &c.
s.f. Ignorance; paganism (see next).

ety: for A. جاهلية, abst. s. fr. jāhilī, fr. jāhil
def: s.f. Ignorance; folly; paganism
subs: The times or days of ignorance or paganism (the times previous to the manifestation of Islām).

def: s.f. A violently flaming or blazing fire; hell-fire, hell.
s.f. corr. of A. jaḥīm, q.v.
adv. In whatsoever place, wherever, wheresoever.

ety: inf. n. of جہل 'to be ignorant'
def: s.m. Ignorance, foolishness, silliness; — foolish or senseless disputation, a war of words.
adj. & s.m. Ignorant; foolish; passionate; lazy; argumentative, disputatious; — an ignoramus; a disputatious person; a driveller.
adj. Of or pertaining to the sect so called; — s.f. (for jahmīyat), The appellation of a Mohammadan sect.
s.f. 'The daughter of Jahnu,' an epithet of the Ganges.

s.m. Name of an ancient king and sage who adopted the Gangā as his daughter. (When the river Ganges was brought down from heaven by the austerities of Bhagīratha, it inundated in its course the sacrificial ground of Jahnu, who there fore drank up its waters, but consented at the praver of Bhagīratha to discharge them from his ears, and hence the river is regarded as his daughter, and is called Jāhnavī.) — jahnu-tanayā, jahnu-sutā, jahnu-kanyā, s.f. 'The daughter of Jahnuʼ, a name of the river Ganges.
s.m. Speaking or reciting with a loud voice, speaking audibly; — jahr or jihar, s.m. A female oblation.
adj. Characterized by great ignorance, very ignorant.
adv. (dialec.) = jahāṅ, q.v.

s.f. Oats; — a small species of barley; small shoots of barley (which are carried about in the turbans of Hindūs during the dasahrā festival); the first sprouts of germinating rice (see jawārā) when the seeds are steeped in water previous to sowing.
adj. & adv. As many as ( = jitnā)
subs: adv. As many times as, as often as.

def: part. adj. & s.f. Born; — a daughter, girl

s.f. The opening at the neck and bosom (of a shirt, &c.); the breast-collar (of a garment); the heart; the bosom; (the Arabs often carry things within the bosom of the shirt, &c.; and hence the word is now applied by them to) 'a pocket' (in which sense the Turks, Persians, and Indians pronounce it jeb)
subs: adj. Having the collar rent; sad
s.m. A pickpocket: jeb katarnā (-kī), To pick the pocket (of)

def: s.f. = jaitī, q.v.

ety: for A. جائفة jāʼifat, fem., rt. جوف
def: s.m. A wound or stab that reaches to the belly (jauf), or to any other vital part; a stab (punishable by a fine).

s.m. A Jain; a teacher of heterodox notions; a sect of Hindus who are worshippers of a number of Jins or great saints whom they regard as superior to the Hindu gods (they also deny the divine authority of the Vedas, and disregard the distinctions of caste)
subs: adj. Of or belonging to the Jain religion; — s.m. A Jain.

def: adj. Of or relating to the Jain sect; of a Jain.
adv. (prov.) = jaisā, q.v.

adj. & adv. (f. -ī), Which-like, what-like; like as, as, such as; in the manner which, according as; — jaise, adv. As, in like manner as, just as, in such kind as, as if: jaise bane, jaise bane taise, adv. As well as is possible, as best you can; anyhow; at any rate
subs: adv. As well as, so so
adv. Somehow or other, by hook or by crook, with difficulty, painfully
As it should be, as it ought (to be), sufficiently; properly, thoroughly, effectually; as may be desired, as occasion may require
jaise-kā taisā, jaise-kā waisā, adv. Such as in the original, as before, as it was (or as I was, as you were); the self-same, unaltered
conj. As though, as if; according to
Like for like, measure for measure, tit for tat, a Rowland for an Oliver
jaise-hī, adv. Just as, in precisely the same manner in which.

def: adv. a dial. var. = jaisā.
s.m. (?), Ebullition; raging (as of the sea, &c.); mental excitement; — an army, a legion, troops.
s.m. Name of certain low-caste tribes.
s.m. Name of certain low-caste tribes.
s.f. A kind of weed (Euphorbia) which springs up with the rabīʻ or spring crops, and yields a kind of oil.
adj. & s.m. Victorious, triumphant, overcoming, surpassing, superior; successful; — a conqueror, victor
subs: adj. & s.m. Having a triumphal car; — a victor, conqueror, hero; — a triumphal car.

def: adj. & s.m. Victorious, triumphant, overcoming, surpassing, superior; successful; — a conqueror, victor
subs: adj. & s.m. Having a triumphal car; — a victor, conqueror, hero; — a triumphal car.

def: s.f. Victrix; a heroine.
s.m. Name of a clan of Rājpūts; — the head inhabitants of a village.
adj. = jān-hār, q.v.
adj. Good, approvable, excellent; elegant; arable, tertile.

adj. Allowable, permitted, possible; passing as right; lawful, right, proper, sound, valid, good (in law), authorized, warrantable; admissible, receivable, competent; — s.m. A joist, beam, the piece of wood which passes across between two walls ( = P. tīr)
subs: adj. Admitting of objection, abjectionable, exceptionable
(-ko), To allow, admit, receive as right, lawful, or valid, &c.; to authorize, warrant, legalize, justify, tolerate
(-ko), To decide to be lawful, &c.; to legalize; to uphold
wety: Borrowed from Arabic جَائِز (jāʔiz).

ety: for A. جائزة jāʼizat, fem. of jāʼiz
def: s.m. Examination, review, verification, confirmation, checking (an account); signature; the mark made in examining or checking; muster; — a gift, present, reward
subs: To undergo examination or scrutiny, to render an account (of one's charge)

def: s.m. (Old H.) Oblation, sacrifice offering.
wety: Borrowed from English judge.

def: s.m. A judge (in law).
wety: Borrowed from English judge.

def: s.m. A sacrificer, sacrificing priest, a priest officiating at a sacrifice.
s.m. A player on cymbals or on the tabor; a wandering minstrel.
s.m. A certain school of the Atharva Veda.
s.f. A chequered or ornamented linen cloth spread over a carpet; floor-cloth ( = jāzam).
s.m. Conducting a sacrifice, causing or assisting (others) to perform sacrifice, sacrificing.
adj. & s.m. = jarjar, q.v.
s.f. The series of binary combinations of letters beginning with jīm (the first being jīm-alif or jā, the second jīm-te or jat).
s.m. A player on cymbals or on the tabor; a wandering minstrel.

s.m. A person who institutes the performance of a sacrifice, employs priests for the purpose, and pays the expenses of it; a customer, a client, one who has a right to certain services (as those of the Brāhman or priest, or the barber, or shoemaker, or washerman, &c.); a client to whose custom Brāhmans, barbers, &c. have a prescriptive claim (the hereditary priest, or barber, &c., of a village must be paid his fees whether you choose to employ him or another person).
s.f. The status and the occupation of a jajmān; the stipend paid by a jajmān; — (S. yajamānī) the wife of a jajmān.
s.m. Wrath, rage, fury.

ety: S. जाज्वलितः, fr. intens. of rt. ज्वल्
def: part. adj. (Old. H.), Inflamed with wrath, enraged, furious.

part. adj. & s.m. Fit to be offered in sacrifice; to be effected by sacrifice; to be sacrificed, sacrificial; — one on whose behalf a sacrifice is made; a sacrificer; the person for whom a sacrifice is performed, the master or institutor of a sacrifice; property or presents obtained by officiating at sacrifices.
s.m. (?), The fifteenth night of the month Shǒʻbān (syn. shab-ě-barāt).
s.m. The animal which is killed and buried along with treasure as a guard; the guard of buried treasure; (met.) a miser (also called jak-kā gumāshta).
s.m. incorr. for next, q.v.

s.m. A deposit or pledge left with a vender for goods brought away for inspection or approval; goods taken from a shop for approval, a deposit or pledge being left; a conditional purchase; articles taken on commission sale; — adv. On inspection, for approval
subs: s.f. Account book of sales subject to approval of goods, &c.
v.t. To sell conditionally, or subject to approval
v.t. To take away goods on inspection, or for approval, leaving a deposit or pledge with the vender.

def: s.m. See s.v. ज ja.
adj. Tight, fast bound, well-strung; — struck with cold or rheumatism.
adv. Tight, tightly
adv. Tight, tightly
v.t. To draw tight (as a knot, &c.), to tighten, to tie tight; to bind, to fasten, to pinion; to put in the stocks
subs: v.n. To become stiff or rigid; to be firmly or tightly bound; to be closely joined or placed together, to be closely packed.

def: s.f. A kind of coarse woolen cloth.
s.f. A jacket.
adj. (prov.) = jhakāwant, q.v.
s.m. A kind of demi-god, attendant especially on Kuvera, and employed in the care of his gardens and treasures (cf. jak).
s.m. The wooden foundation of the brickwork of a well.
adv. (prov. — correl. takhan), What time, when; at whatever time.
s.m. Name of a subdivision of the jāṭ tribe.
s.m. A mass or heap of lumber or rubbish (especially such as is piled up to be burnt).
s.f. Wife of a jakh, q.v.; a sort of female fiend attached to the service of Durgā, and frequently maintaining, like a sylph or fairy, an intercourse with mortals.
adj. & s.m. (prov.) = jhakī; and jhakkī; qq.v.
s.m. A kind of demi-god, attendant especially on Kuvera, and employed in the care of his gardens and treasures (cf. jak).

s.m. A net (for catching birds, fish, &c.); net-work; reticulated or chain armour, a coat of mail; — grating; trellis-work; a lattice; an eyelet; a loop-hole; a window; — a sash; — magic, conjuring; illusion, deception; supernatural appearance; — the tree Salvador indica
subs: To spread a net; to lay or set a trap (for, -ke wāst̤e)
adj. & s.m. Reticulated; — net-work; muslin, nainsukh, or other fine cloth embroidered with the semblance of the scales of fish, &c.
To cast a net (for fish); to set a trap (for a person)
jāl-meṅ ḍālnā, jāl-meṅ lānā (-ko), To take in a net, to ensuare, entrap, inveigle
adj. & s.m. Wearing a coat of mail; — one so covered.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit जाल (jāla). Cognate with Assamese জাল (zal), Punjabi ਜਾਲ਼ (jāḷ), Sinhalese දැල (dæla).

def: adj. corr. of جعل jaʻl, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit जाल (jāla). Cognate with Assamese জাল (zal), Punjabi ਜਾਲ਼ (jāḷ), Sinhalese දැල (dæla).

ety: inf. n. of جعل 'to make a thing'
def: s.m. A fabrication, a counterfeit; forgery
subs: (-kā), To make an illicit imitation (of), to fabricate, to forge
s.m. One who counterfeits; a forger
adv. By fraudulent means, corruptly, dish mestly
v.n. To commit forgery, to forge.

def: s.m. A net; a web, spider's web, cobweb: a pellicle; a speck (in the eye), cataract; a large jar, an earthen vessel; mildew (on flour, &c.); a kind of water-weed (used in refining sugar)
subs: v.n. To be overgrown with weeds.

def: s.m. Water; — frigidity (physical or moral = jaṛ); — a kind of fragrant medicinal plant; — adj. Cold; apathetic; stupid, idiotic ( = jaṛ); — in the ingenious system of reckoning by symbols adopted by Hindūs in their poetry, this word is a symbol for four
subs: (jala+āć˚), s.m. Rinsing the mouth with water
s.m. A pond, lake, reservoir, any receptacle of water
(jala+ar˚), s.m. The sea of fresh water; the rainy season; great flood, deluge, inundation
vulg. jalāsā (jala+āśaya), s.m. A pond, tank, lake, reservoir, any piece of water; a kind of fragrant grass, Andropogon muricatus; — adj. Cold, apathetic; dull, stupid
(jala+ak˚), s.m. Appearance of water; source of water, spring, fountain
jalālokā (jala+āl˚), s.f. 'Living in water'; a leech
(jala+maya), adj. Consisting of water; abounding in water, full of water; watery, wet
(jala+an˚), s.m. A spring, well; a natural water-course; — the plant Blyxa octandra
s.m. The Persian wheel (for raising water)
s.m. A bubble of water; — jal-brahmī, s.f. A kind of pot-herb, Hingcha repens
s.m. 'Water-barrier'; a dam, dike, rocks or stones impeding a current
s.m. lit. 'Produced in or by water'; a cloud; a kind of aquatic plant, Commelina salicifolia or Bengalensis
s.m. Swimming, striking with the arms and legs in water
s.m. 'Drinking water'; any slight repast, refreshment, luncheon; — jal-pān karnā, To take some refreshment, to drink
s.f. An aquatic plant, Commelina salicifolia, and another species
s.m. 'Lord of the waters,' Varuṇa or the Hindū Neptune; the ocean
adj. & s.m. Abounding with water; — a country or place abounding with water
s.m., jal-pṛishṭhajā, s.f. The plant Blyxa octandra, or Pistia stratiotes
s.m. Destruction by water; the destruction of the world by water
adj. & s.m. Fond of water; frequenting water; — the bird ćātak, Cuculus melanoleucus; a fish
jal-tāl, s.m. The hilsā or sable fish, Clupanodon ilisha

s.f. jal-tarang, s.m., jal-tarangī, s.f. A metal cup filled with water, the edges of which when beaten with two sticks produce harmonical notes; the musical glasses or harmonicon; playing on glasses or China bowls by rubbing the edges
jal-ṭhal, s.m. Ground half-covered with water, marshy ground; a sheet of water; any place where water settles or abounds
jalaj, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Produced or born in water; growing or living in water; — an aquatic animal; a fish; a shell; a lotus
s.f. A species of plant (Bassia) growing in or near water
s.f. A journey by water, a voyage
adj. Full of water; watery, wet
s.m. A fish; any marine or aquatic or amphibious animal
jal-ćar, adj. & s.m. Going in water; living in or near water; aquatic; amphibious; an aquatic animal
s.m. A stand or place where water is supplied to travellers
s.m. Sprinkling or splashing with water, a splash
s.m. lit. 'Giving or pouring forth water'; a cloud; a fragrant grass, Cyperus rotundus; name of a varsh or division of the known continent
s.m. The tree Shorea robusta (syn. sāl)
s.m. Giving or supplying with water
s.f. A stream or current of water
adj. & s.m. Holding or carrying or having water; — a cloud; the ocean; a kind of fragrant grass, Cyperus rotundus
jal-rāsī, s.m. A quantity of water, a sheet of water; the ocean
jal-rank, s.m. A species of vak or crane, Ardea nivea
s.m. lit. 'Growing in water'; the lotus
s.m. Touching water (esp. Ganges water) for religious purposes
s.m. Ceremonial purification by means of the external application of water
(S. jala+sūtra), s.m. The guinea worm, Filaria medinensis (syn. nahāruʼā)
s.f. The Gangetic porpoise, Delphinus gangeticus; a fish, a small sort of pike, Esox cancila; a leech; an aquatic plant, Trapa bispinosa
(S. jala+śayinī), s.f. A fish; sleeping in water by way of mortification; drowning by sleeping in water as an act of penance

jal-śāyī, jal-śay, jal-śayan, adj. 'Sleeping on the water, an epithet of Vishnu (who is supposed to sleep, reposing on his serpent-couch above the waters, during the four months of the periodical rains, and also during the intervals of the submersion of the world)
jal-kāg, s.m. 'Water-crow'; the diver; the cormorant, Corvus marinus
s.m. lit. 'Desirous of water'; an elephant
s.m. Rent or tax derived from water for fisheries, &c.; revenue assessed on fisheries, &c.
s.m. A shark; a large alligator
s.f. Sporting or gamboling in water; bathing for pleasure or amusement
adj. & s.m. Dry, parched; — scarcity of water, drought
jal-kukaṛ, jal-kukkaṛ, s.m. (f. -ī), The common water-hen, Gallinula chloropus; the diver; the coot
s.m. lit. 'Water-hair'; the aquatic plant Blyxa octandra
s.m. 'Water-thorn'; a crocodile; the plant Trapa bispinosa
s.m. A spring; a pool of water that never dries up
s.f. A spring, well; pond, pool; a whirlpool
adj. 'Destroyed by water'; dried up, parched (soil, &c.)
s.m. lit. 'Water-hair'; the plant Blyxa octandra
s.m. A turtle, tortoise; a quadrangular tank, a piece of water; a whirlpool
s.m. Watercourse, a drain or canal leading from a pool, &c.
s.m. A merman, the water-kelpie; a small species of monkey

jal-mandar, s.m. A house or fabric erected in the midst of water (commonly used as a summer-house); subterranean apartments constructed in the bank of a river to serve as a retreat in the hot season; a summer-house
s.m. A kind of tarantula or large spider (the bite of which is said to be fatal)
s.m. An epithet of the god Śiva (of whom one form is water, implying his presence in all matter)
adj. Abounding in water; consisting of water; watery; deluged; — s.m. Deluge; inundation
s.m. 'Treasure of water'; the ocean, sea
s.m. A drain, watercourse; a pipe along a wall or building for carrying off water; a water-fall, the descent of a spring, &c. into a river below
s.f. The aquatic plant Blyxa octandra
s.m. A species of bitter herb, Herpestes monniera, which grows on the banks of ponds, &c., and is used as a drug
(jala+ut˚), s.m. The ceremony connected with the marrying of a pond, well, &c.
(jala+uć˚), s.m. A drain or channel made for carrying off an excess of water, a similar channel made naturally by the overflow of a river, &c.; a conduit
(jala+ud˚), s.m. 'Water-belly.'; dropsy (pop. jalandhar); a dropsical person
(jala+ud˚), s.m. The course of the water
s.m. The autumnal equinox, the moment of the sun's entering Libra
jalaukas (jala+ok˚), s.f. 'Living in water'; a leech
s.m. A drop of water; name of a place of Hindū pilgrimage
s.m. Sporting or gamboling in water
s.m. A kind of pike-fish, Esox cancila
jalah, s.m. A summer-house; a small piece of water, a pond, lake
s.m. Cuttle-fish bone (considered as the indurated foam of the sea)

(jala+iś˚), s.m. 'Lord of waters'; the sea, the ocean; the deity of the waters, Varuṇa; — jal-yantra, s.m. A water-engine, a machine for raising water, any contrivance connected with that element, water-works; a water-clock, clepsydra
ex:
جل میں ٹھہرا ترا مرا چہرا
کیسا دریا تھا وہ سبھا کیسی
jal mẽ ṭhahrā tirā mirā cehrā
kaisā dariyā thā voh sabhā kaisī
(please add an English translation of this quotation)
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit जल (jala).

def: s.m. A mass of water, a lake, tank, reservoir; — a shell, a cowrie
subs: s.m. A boy's game, played with cowries (somewhat like the English game of ring-taw; — also written jhallā-mīrī).
